title: Anatomy of the Shoulder Joint (Glenohumeral Joint)
description: OpenStax diagram of a coronal cross-section through the glenohumeral joint. It clearly illustrates the articulating surfaces (humerus and glenoid cavity), the stabilizing labrum, the joint capsule, and the crucial spatial relationship of the subacromial bursa and supraspinatus tendon beneath the acromion.
clinical_notes: Subacromial impingement syndrome occurs when the supraspinatus tendon and subacromial bursa are compressed between the humeral head and the coracoacromial arch during arm elevation. OT focuses on modifying overhead activities, ergonomic setup, and strengthening scapular stabilizers to improve subacromial space clearance.
